definition.name: square-hole
definition.definition: def-square-hole (definitions/def-square-hole.md): For r,s in A=Im P, the PRODUCT HOLE is h_{r,s}:=r o s - P(r o s) in Ker P; the POLARISED/square hole is q_{r,s}:=P(r o s) - r o s in Ker P. Hence h_{r,s} = -q_{r,s} EXACTLY (negatives of each other), so ||h_{r,s}||=||q_{r,s}|| and P(h_{r,s} o z)=-P(q_{r,s} o z) for any z; |P(h o z)| bounds equal |P(q o z)| bounds. Both lie in Ker P (P(r o s)=P(r o s), so P(h_{r,s})=P(r o s)-P(P(r o s))=P(r o s)-P(r o s)=0 by P^2=P).
